pageTitle.value: Hans Magnus Enzensberger and Tracy K. Smith - Video

pageCaption.value: In a mentorship that they describe as an intense and joyful conversation, poets Hans Magnus Enzensberger (from Germany) and Tracy K. Smith (American) explore the nature of poetry – “Everyone has poetry in their head,” Enzensberger proclaims. But their quest goes further, examining history and identity as Smith realizes a longstanding desire to write her family memoir.

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégée in Literature, 2010 - 2011

title.value: Hans Magnus Enzensberger and Tracy K. Smith

text.value: In a mentorship that they describe as an intense and joyful conversation, poets Hans Magnus Enzensberger (from Germany) and Tracy K. Smith (American) explore the nature of poetry – “Everyone has poetry in their head,” Enzensberger proclaims. But their quest goes further, examining history and identity as Smith realizes a longstanding desire to write her family memoir.

pageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

pageCaption.value: Declaring their mentorship to be “a process of development”, Wole Soyinka, first African Laureate of the Nobel Prize for Literature, encourages his protégée, Tara June Winch, as she agonizes over her writing. When anyone writes, he says, himself included, “it’s all over the place.” It’s the rewriting that follows that marks out a true writer.

WaPageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

pageTitle.value: Mia Couto and Julián Fuks - Video

pageCaption.value: Mia Couto’s mentoring relationship with Julián Fuks was a fusion of interests wrought by their mutual experiences as writers living in former colonies of Portugal. Liberated from his “autofiction” style of writing by his mentor, Fuks found he was able to explore his imagination in a different way.

pageCaption.value: Irish writer Colin Barrett recognizes that writing is a solitary pursuit, but also acknowledges the need to escape solitude and meet other people and fellow authors.

metadescription.value: Irish writer Colin Barrett recognizes that writing is a solitary pursuit, but also acknowledges the need to escape solitude and meet other people and fellow authors.

pageTitle.value: Brian Eno and Ben Frost - Video

pageCaption.value: As they create a track of electronic music during their mentoring year, multimedia artists Brian Eno and Ben Frost reveal the two sides of composition, finding inspiration and analyzing the music they create and how it will be heard.

pageTitle.value: Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib - Video

pageCaption.value: With a live performance and both Chicago and Beirut as the backdrop, Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib give surprising – and very different – explanations of the nature of art, as well as describing each other, their mentorship and how to change the world.

WaPageTitle.value: Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib - Video

pageTitle.value: Anish Kapoor and Nicholas Hlobo - Video

pageCaption.value: In a mentorship that is a process, rather than a collaboration on a joint work, Nicholas Hlobo’s art, which springs from many hours of precise handicraft, is contrasted with the monumental pieces created by his mentor Anish Kapoor, that seem “to be made by the gods”.
